item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have no idea what it means. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: yassss

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: stephlynch did the unpossible

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: the new images are fantastic

item.title: SCP-1918 2.0
item.content:encoded:
• Edited article for clarity.
  • Added two images by stephlynch which have been lightly edited by me.
    • Original Artifact image and original hallway image.
  • Added a more clear connection to the other SCP this is connected to as was intended.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I concur to your concurring, I did not get the punch at the end, or whatever the comic was with the D’s head. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Haha, SCP-1918 is one sore loser. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For some reason I didn't think it explained itself well enough and gave it -1, but one night I just remembered how much I liked the unnerving underground imagery of the article and had to find it to give it a +1 instead.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "What the fuck-ever Pez Dispenser mothefucker." is one of the best sentences on this entire site. I can't believe I never +1'd this before, so that's been remedied.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Someone used him in a tale or scp, I forget which it was. It's intentionally ambiguous.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The D class won fairly. The pdmf as people are calling it is just a bit of a stick.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Don't know or care what this is but it's fucking creepy as shit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I tried rereading this again and uh, basically everything is poorly explained or weirdly organized and I don't know what's going on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The camera was chest mounted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Did… did he win?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. Sorry, I meant to imply the trail was grinding. Just, carvings, grinding. No liquid or anything weird. Will fix.
  2. Yes, they need to make a mark. No way of making a mark is given, but people have other ways of making a mark.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: PDMF is my new favorite acronym. I didn't realize what you were talking about for awhile. My question about this is thus: Does activating it guarantee death by PDMF, or did I misread and there's an escape method?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this. It's weird, it's relatively well-done, but I do have a couple of nit-picks that are keeping it out of +1 territory for me.
First, it is mentioned that the PDMF writes messages "in its trail" but no mention is made of said trail in the description of the PDMF (or anywhere but the mention of messages). Is it leaving some substance behind, or does it just spell out words that people are inferring solely from its motions? Is it scratching the floor, maybe? Though that would become less effective after a while with layers of scratching.
Second is the bit people have mentioned where the Tic-Tac-Toe part mentions no way to make a mark, but Memory mentioned that the subject has to mark an O. Maybe put in both that an O is needed and after both put that no way of making a mark is provided to include both in that statement?

That being said, the existence but apparent lack of purpose of the 2 rooms intrigues me. Has anyone tried entering the central 2 room without entering the central 1 room? Maybe a different PDMF with different games?

item.title: *snerk*
item.content:encoded:
"D-2934: Okay. Okay. What the fuck ever Pez Dispenser mothefucker."

I honestly didn't think much of the SCP, but that alone deserves an upvote. Its not often an SCP makes me chortle like that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The D in the test log cheated by getting to the rooms and marking them "O" before the SCP could mark any of them "X", hence cheating. How it cheated on memoree, I don't know. Maybe by going to the central room again and marking it "O", instead of trying to find it's match. Either way he lost.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Pez Dispenser Motherfucker"
Congrats, you've now beaten Red vs Blue's "Dickbiscuit" for best insult. Take my upvote, good sir.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+1
I don't even

item.title: sooooo mannnnnny upvotes
item.content:encoded: take my +1 and my newly found fear of pole's.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I concur, usually the logs add a clarification, but in this case there is only further obfuscation. -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes sir!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It definitely made for an interesting read, and the last bit was rather terrifying, but there were so many parts where the description seemed repetitive and confusing, I had to give it -1.

The experiment log was amazing though. It really grabbed me at the end! Good job!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't even
But I like

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I get the impression that after he broke the "head" of the PDMF/1918, the body somehow incapacitated him (probably using the same mechanism behind "memoree") and took the D-class' head for its own. The camera is still attached to the decapitated D-class torso, thus facing the ceiling - the 'leaning forward to look at the camera' is 1918 leaning forward ("The object leans in the direction it travels in"), now with the D-class head atop it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The documentation mentions "new game" but not "cheat". I thought the implication was that the particular D in the log did something specific the PDMF didn't approve of.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Out of curiosity, what were you trying to imply happened to the D-class?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure if I'm understanding that feedback completely but what I'm implying is that they would have to use bodily fluid to mark the X's and O's…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It doesn't activate unless you go into the middle room, and the only cameras mentioned in the article are the ones mounted on the chest of the test subject, and one "recovered on the surface".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, nothing's said about what happens if multiple people get in there at once… After all, it seems there is, in fact, only one Pez Dispenser Motherfucker.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I read it. Despite one or two quibbles mentioned above (specifically the whole "should be marked but has no way to do so" thing) I upvoted it. Then I got to this line.

What the fuck ever Pez Dispenser mothefucker.

Then I wanted to upvote it again and couldn't. Dammit, Wikidot!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvoted for a few reasons. First, the fact that the instructions for "Tik Tak Tow" specifically mention that there is nothing provided to write with, but that "Memoree" also requires a participant to write, yet the lack of a writing implement is never mentioned. If it's a problem for one game it should be for both. Also, this is the type of thing that should have pages and pages of test logs. Considering they seem to know a lot about how the game works they must have sacrificed hundreds of people to determine what needs to be drawn where and how the basic rules work since there are no apparent instructions. It would have been all trial and error. I also really don't like how this is basically a black hole that kills people. You can't leave the game or you die, and even if you win it forces you to keep playing until you lose, at which point you die. Which begs the question: how did the camera get into the facility if people die after every event, you can't leave without playing, and the facility "resets" after every event.

Needs some cleaning up to address these issues.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is very cool. Liked the Pez Dispensers line, the image, idea… the stuff happening at the end really made this SCP. There were a few grammar errors (I think a missing comma or two), and, if there is a SCP-XX-2, should there be a SCP-XX-1? +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think I'm going to upvote for both the concept and the fact that that picture is creepy as fuck.

Also for "Pez dispenser motherfucker".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The facility is underground, nothing buried it there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What's this doing there?

and shows no sign of an unnatural burial.

Leaving aside the question of what a "natural" burial would be, it doesn't quite seem right. It sounds like you're saying the researchers thought it might be a ghost, but that seems a very un-Foundation-like hypothesis. (Apart from anything else, "paranormal activity caused by an unquiet spirit" is a terrible cliche.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this so I upvoted it.

I do have some issues with it though. The fact that subjects just disappear if they fail to cooperate stands out as odd, somehow, and I'm not entirely sure how this thing can consistently knock people out for the game of memoree when it moves slow and is basically a stick. If the subject in the test log can (apparently) take it down fairly easily with a pipe, that makes it even stranger to me.

But that's just me being nitpicky. I enjoyed it. I like SCPs that don't have a tacked on "…or else it murders you" or a diary that ends in "FLESH TEAR FLESH HATE BLOOD" or whatever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I prefer to think that the D-class won and escaped, and is currently at large.

… I hate to say this but I think I am voting for the line about pez dispensers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At the end… Did it take the D's head?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, unless they bring something in with them there's no way to mark. Will change speed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm crazy. And also hard to please today, it seems.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You're crazy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty good idea. A couple things.

The speed has already been mentioned, but "1 kilometer per 12 minutes" seems pretty nonstandard. Just 5 kph would be fine.

Also, in Memoree, you win if you mark an O, but in Tik Tak Tow it's stated there's no way to mark anything. That seems an oversight.

The image caption made me think this is a rewrite of the Gastly from yesterday. Am I crazy?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a matching game. You're shown one card (the central room) and then knocked out, and you have to find the matching card and "select it".

I think my favorite part about this is that whatever it is seems to automatically assume that if the other person won, they cheated. It really adds to the creepy childlike nature of this thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This one's all kinds of weird. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't quite understand the rules of 'memoree', but other than that, it's excellent. And the end is just plain creepy. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: oh god

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I forget where I found it, and a reverse image search brings up nothing.

Some art exhibit thing on reddit.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is so good, but I can't help but wonder what the source of the image is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Weird with a touch of creepy. I agree with Scantron about the 12 minutes per kilometer issue, but I like the rest of it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Fixed, I think.

Can't help you with that other thing. Going back to read the comment, I do not know why the fuck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
moves at a set speed of 12 minutes per kilometer

That's not speed. Its speed is 5 kph. In other words, I don't need to know how long it takes this thing to move a kilometer, I need to know how far it can move in a given timeframe.

Having now gone back and read this, I don't even know what the fuck, and I can't stand the D-class's dialogue.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: …I don't get it. It's strange, but I honestly can't quite tell what's going on. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Probably my favorite one out of the 30 or so I've read today, and I've been reading some pretty good ones. Definite +1

The only way I could like it better is if there was perhaps a little bite-size tidbit of information to give our minds a path or two to jump on when asking "why?" on a lot of things…. for example, it's stated that attempts to remove it have been unsuccessful, but it's not really clear to me why they would be unsuccessful. The end makes it sound like the "thing" can be hurt (although I'm sure it's more complicated than that) and also alludes to the fact that canines set the thing off too. In terms of a more creepypasta style I could see it being left out, which is fine, but on the scientific side it's curious that they'd mention all of these things at the start of the report and just never say anything about it again. The ending also sort of bothers me in that it's very creepypasta but not very report-like.

But hell, I'm not even sure if it's a complaint or not. Maybe it's fine. I'm just musing. I really love it but it just feels like it could be a little more epic than it already is.

edit: I guess I could sum up my opinion in a bit more concise phrase, as imagined coming up from a researcher on the project. "It does all this really weird shit, but we had a hockey game to get to so instead of extensive testing we just threw some D-Class at it and let them run around, said 'huh, ain't that something', then left."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very well made +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this a lot, if it was an ice cream treat it would be a sundae.

The ice cream is wonderfully 173 flavoured with just a hint of playful creep, the syrupy drizzle is the Tic Tac Toe weirdness, and the fluffy whipped cream is the playfulness that just surrounds the piece. Not many articles make me crave some ice cream but this is definitely one of them, welcome to my favourite articles you sunovabitch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I thought the picture would help. :P

As for the second question, that was done intentionally as it was meant to be rhetorical.
